summary: Swedish fighters intercepted Russian Su-24 and Su-34 bombers over the Baltic Sea. Russian tactical aviation launched multiple KAB attacks on Vasylkivka, causing civilian injuries and market fires. Ukrainian drones struck infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Krasnodar, prompting Russian authorities to report 177 intercepted drones and damage to the Chongar bridge.
key_points: Swedish fighter jets intercepted Russian Su-24 and Su-34 bombers over the Baltic Sea (Source: @tass_agency/380515).
Ukrainian forces attacked a central market in Svatove, LNR, wounding seven civilians (Source: @tass_agency/380520).
Russia launched 12 KABs on Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk region, injuring nine people and igniting a market fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian drones targeted infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Krasnodar, with Russian officials reporting 177 intercepted drones and fires at the Temryuk sea terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
A bridge at Chongar connecting occupied Crimea and Kherson was damaged, halting traffic through the Janukay checkpoint (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Multiple hostile UAVs were reported across Ukrainian regions, including Chernihiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, and Kherson (Source: @kpszsu/65012, @kpszsu/65007, @kpszsu/65036, @kpszsu/65011).
Threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from occupied Crimea was highlighted by monitoring channels (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67581).
Ukraine imposed sanctions on ten Russian mobile operators and related internet providers operating in occupied territories (Source: @UkraineNow/81895).

sections.executive_summary: Swedish fighters intercepted Russian Su-24 and Su-34 bombers over the Baltic Sea, indicating heightened NATO‑Russia aerial tension. Russian tactical aviation conducted a series of KAB strikes on Vasylkivka, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian drone operations continued deep into occupied Crimea and Krasnodar, prompting Russian reports of massive interceptions and critical bridge damage at Chongar.

points: Ukrainian forces attacked the central market in Svatove, LNR, wounding seven civilians, according to local authorities (Source: @tass_agency/380520).

Ukrainian drones struck infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Russia's Krasnodar region, with Russian officials reporting 177 intercepted drones and fires at the Temryuk sea terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).

Ukrainian UAV activity was reported in Chernihiv (south of Slavutych, heading south‑southeast), Dnipropetrovsk (near Bozhedarivka/Kamianke), and Kharkiv (north‑east of Kharkiv, heading south‑southeast) (Source: @kpszsu/65009, @kpszsu/65017, @kpszsu/65036).
Ukrainian drones were observed over Kherson region moving toward Velyka Oleksandrivka and Beryslav, with air defence assets engaged (Source: @kpszsu/65007).
In the Dnipropetrovsk region, a UAV was sighted near Marhanets after passing through Nikopol district (Source: @kpszsu/65017).

Russian tactical aviation launched 12 KABs on Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk region, injuring nine civilians, igniting a market fire, and damaging multiple shops and a residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Russian forces reported the destruction of a railway bridge at Chongar, a key link between occupied Crimea and Kherson, halting traffic through the Janukay checkpoint (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).

Russian authorities claimed interception of 177 Ukrainian drones across several regions, including Crimea and Krasnodar, after fires were reported at the Temryuk sea terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Russia announced a strategic advantage in the Special Military Operation, stating its forces are advancing on all fronts (Source: @tass_agency/380492).
Russian officials warned of potential Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from occupied Crimea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67581).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported south of Zaporizhzhia moving northward, and in Donetsk region near Marhanets, indicating continued aerial activity on the eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/65011).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the south‑east and south directions targeting Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/65025).
Ukrainian drones attacked a training ground in Zaporizhzhia, targeting Russian personnel, air defence systems, and a missile launcher (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the Donetsk People's Republic, with Russian pilots reportedly destroying temporary dislocation points using FAB‑3000 bombs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Multiple hostile UAVs were reported in Chernihiv region, including near Korivka, Slavutych, and north of Slavutych heading south‑southeast (Source: @kpszsu/65009, @eRadarrua/96415).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted north‑east of Kharkiv, moving south‑southeast, suggesting pressure on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/65036).
A UAV was sighted north of Chernihiv heading toward Slavutych (Source: @kpszsu/65009).
Ukrainian reconnaissance reported UAVs over Sumy region near Putivl and Buryny, indicating northern operational reach (Source: @kpszsu/65042, @kpszsu/65043).
Ukrainian drones were observed in Kherson region moving toward Velyka Oleksandrivka and Beryslav (Source: @kpszsu/65007).
Russian KAB attacks were reported over Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk, causing civilian injuries and market fires (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian UAVs were sighted south of Mykolaiv region heading north, indicating activity along the southern axis (Source: @kpszsu/65011).
Ukrainian forces reported strikes on bridges linking Crimea with the mainland, notably the Chongar bridge (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Swedish fighter jets intercepted Russian Su‑24 and Su‑34 bombers over the Baltic Sea, demonstrating NATO air engagement with Russian assets (Source: @tass_agency/380515).
Russian tactical aviation launched multiple KAB attacks on Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk, with Ukrainian air defence reporting 110 of 118 attacking drones neutralised (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian drones conducted deep strikes in occupied Crimea and Krasnodar, prompting Russian reports of 177 interceptions and fires at the Temryuk terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).

Ukrainian UAVs were observed over multiple front‑line regions, including Chernihiv, Kharkiv, and Kherson, indicating widespread aerial reconnaissance and strike activity (Source: @kpszsu/65012, @kpszsu/65036, @kpszsu/65007).
Russian forces claimed strategic air superiority and continued advances across all directions (Source: @tass_agency/380492).

key_developments: Swedish fighters intercepted Russian Su‑24 and Su‑34 bombers over the Baltic Sea, highlighting NATO‑Russia aerial tension. (Source: @tass_agency/380515)
Russian tactical aviation launched 12 KABs on Vasylkivka, injuring nine civilians and igniting a market fire. (Source: @UkraineNow/81890)
Ukrainian drones struck infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Krasnodar; Russian officials reported 177 intercepted drones and fires at Temryuk sea terminal. (Source: @eRadarrua/96377)
A bridge at Chongar linking occupied Crimea and Kherson was damaged, halting traffic through the Janukay checkpoint. (Source: @eRadarrua/96377)

Multiple hostile UAVs were reported across Ukrainian regions, including Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson, indicating sustained aerial pressure. (Source: @kpszsu/65012, @kpszsu/65036, @kpszsu/65007)
Threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from occupied Crimea was flagged by monitoring channels. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67581)
Ukraine imposed sanctions on ten Russian mobile operators and related internet providers operating in occupied territories. (Source: @UkraineNow/81895)
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial assets continue to operate near Ukraine's borders, as evidenced by the interception of Su‑24 and Su‑34 bombers by Swedish fighters. Ukraine's expanding UAV campaign reaches deep into occupied Crimea and Russian territory, prompting Russian claims of massive interceptions but also causing infrastructure damage such as the Chongar bridge. The reported threat of Iskander‑M missile launches from Crimea adds a strategic ballistic dimension, while Russian KAB strikes on civilian targets in the south increase the risk of escalation and civilian casualties.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian forces are intensifying drone operations across the northern, eastern, and southern fronts, targeting logistics hubs and command points. Russian air defence reports high interception rates, yet continued KAB attacks on civilian infrastructure suggest a willingness to employ indiscriminate tactics. NATO's involvement, illustrated by Swedish fighter interceptions, indicates broader regional vigilance and potential for coordinated air defence support.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring channels consistently report UAV movements, providing granular data on flight paths and target areas, useful for predictive modelling of future strike zones. The high number of intercepted drones reported by Russian authorities may be inflated; independent verification is needed. Sanctions on Russian telecom operators could degrade Russian command‑and‑control communications in occupied zones, potentially affecting coordination of missile and drone attacks.
